# The sound generated by a fast parton in the quark-gluon plasma is a crescendo

> The total energy deposited into the medium per unit length by a fast parton traversing a quark-gluon plasma is calculated. We take the medium excitation due to collisions to be given by the well known expression for the collisional drag force. The parton's radiative energy loss contributes to the energy deposition because each radiated gluon acts as an additional source of collisional energy loss in the medium. In our model, this leads to a length dependence on the differential energy loss due to the interactions of radiated gluons with the medium. The final result, which is a sum of the primary and the secondary contributions, is then treated as the coefficient of a local hydrodynamic source term. Results are presented for energy density wave induced by two fast, back-to-back partons created in an initial hard interaction.

## Key claim
The total energy deposited into the quark-gluon plasma per unit length by a fast parton includes both the primary collisional energy loss and the collisional energy loss of radiated gluons, leading to a length-dependent growth of the medium excitation that the abstract describes as a "crescendo."

## Method
The authors calculated the collisional energy loss of a fast parton using the standard expression for the drag force, then derived an evolution equation for the spectrum of radiated gluons in the medium, accounting for their subsequent collisional energy loss. The gluon radiation spectrum was taken from the multiple soft scattering approximation of Salgado and Wiedemann (2003). The total energy deposition per unit length was then used as a local hydrodynamic source term in linearized hydrodynamics to compute the energy density perturbation in the medium.

## Key numbers
- For a primary fast quark with parameters α_s = 1/π, T = 300 MeV, and E_p = 50 GeV, the radiative contribution to the energy deposition per unit length was fitted as (dE/dt)_R ≈ 0.3 GeV/fm + 0.364 t GeV/fm² for t < 6 fm.
- The Debye mass was taken as m_D = gT, with g related to α_s by α_s = g²/4π.
- The jet quenching parameter was given by q̂ = 2 α_s C₂ m_D² T ln(2√(E_p T)/m_D).
- The medium response was calculated at time t = 8 fm for back-to-back quarks with energy E_p = 50 GeV, using η/s = 0.2 and c_s/c = 0.57.

## Context
This work extends prior studies of how the quark-gluon plasma responds to fast partons, which were motivated by measurements of hadron correlation functions consistent with conical emission patterns (Adams 2005, Adler 2005, Ulery 2007, Adare 2008). It builds on theoretical studies of medium response (Machcone, Renk 2005, Betz 2008, Neufeld 2008) and addresses the same problem as recent work by Qin et al. (2009). The results enable more realistic modeling of Mach cone formation in heavy-ion collisions by including the path-length-dependent contribution from radiated gluons.
